modules.name: shrunk.dev_logins
documentation:
Development logins. We have one DEV_* user for each user class
(regular, facstaff, power user, admin).

modules.name: shrunk.ci-test-config
documentation:
shrunk - Rutgers University URL Shortener

Configuration options for shrunk. This is an example file; modify this and save
it as config.py.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.DB_HOST
documentation: The host machine for the database.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.DB_PORT
documentation: The database's port on the host machine.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.GEOLITE_PATH
documentation: The path to the geolite geoip database.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.SECRET_KEY
documentation: A secret key for Flask.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.SHRUNK_URL
documentation: The public URL for shrunk.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.LINKSERVER_URL
documentation: The public URL for the link server.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.DUAL_SERVER
documentation:
Determines whether or not the web application server doubles as the link
server. The default setting is False, which assumes that a separate server
handles the links.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.USER_WHITELIST
documentation:
These users are always permitted to login (i.e. they can't be blacklisted)
and are automatically admins.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.SSO_ATTRIBUTE_MAP
documentation: Map SSO attributes to session keys

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.SSO_LOGIN_URL
documentation: URL for local shibboleth login

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.LOG_FORMAT
documentation: The format for the logger.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.LOG_FILENAME
documentation: The name of the log file.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.MAX_DISPLAY_LINKS
documentation: The maximum number of links to display per page.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.MAX_VISITS_FOR_CSV
documentation:
The maximum number of visits to allow when generating a CSV file from search
results. If a CSV would have more than this number of visits, an error is returned.
This restriction does not apply to CSV files generated from a single short URL.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.LDAP_VALIDATE_NETIDS
documentation: Whether to query the LDAP server to validate netids.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.RESERVED_WORDS
documentation:
Words that cannot be used as shortened URLs. Names used as routes are automatically
disallowed, and do not need to be added to this list.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.SECURITY_MEASURES_ON
documentation:
Whether the security measures are turned on or off.
You would this to be set to True when performing unit tests
or you'll fail the security tests. However, in prod it can be whatever.

variables.name: shrunk.ci-test-config.GITHUB_OUTLOOK_WEBHOOK_UPDATE_SECRET
documentation:
The secret for authenticating the webhook API for updating
the Outlook Add-In version so that only GitHub Webhooks
can make the scripts run to download new version of Outlook Add-In

classes.name: shrunk.client.positions.PositionClient

methods.name: shrunk.client.positions.PositionClient.get_position_info
documentation:
Get the position info for a user needed to make role request decisions.

> ```
> param entity
>
> The entity to get the position info for.
>
> returns Dict[str, List[str]]
>
> The role request position info for the entity. It is formatted as follows:
> ```
>
> ```json
>
> ```

{

: "uid": List[str],
  "rutgersEduStaffDepartment": List[str],
  "title": List[str],
  "employeeType": List[str],

}
This class implements the Shrunk position retrieval functionality.

documentation:
Get the default text that apears in a role menu.

```

Role name
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.roles.RolesClient.create
documentation:
---
param custom_text: custom text to show on the form. see {py:func}`_default_text` source
  for options
param oncreate: callback for extra logic when granting a role, e.g. remove a user's
  links on ban
param onrevoke: callback for extra logic when revoking a role, e.g. reenabling a user's
  link when they are unbanned
param process_entity: callback to transform entity before it's inserted into the db
param qualifier_func: takes in a netid and returns whether or not a user is qualified
  to add to a specific role.
param role: Role name
param validator_func: |-
  takes in an entity (like netid or link) and returns whether it's valid for a role. for
  example, it could take a link like `htp://fuz1` and say it's not a valid link
---

documentation: Raised when a link has been detected to be a security risk

classes.name: shrunk.client.exceptions.InvalidStateChange
documentation:
Raised when someone changes a state of an entity incorrectly,
perhaps from one state to another state that cannot be reached from
previous state.

classes.name: shrunk.client.exceptions.LinkIsPendingOrRejected
documentation:
If a specific url is pending verification and client tries to verify,
we don't go further and tell the user that either the link is still pending
or it has been rejected.

documentation: raised if a viewer was not an org or netid

documentation: Toggles security feature

methods.name: shrunk.client.security.SecurityClient.get_security_status

methods.name: shrunk.client.security.SecurityClient.security_risk_detected
documentation:
Checks a url with a security risk API. In this case,
the API is Google Safe Browsing API. For now, if the status
code is not 200 when making a request, we continue with the link
creation.

The daily quota for the Lookup API is 10,000. If Google Safe Browsing
API returns an error, this method will return false no matter what.
This is to ensure that link creation continues despite Google Safe
Browsing API failure.

```
param long_url

a long url to verify
```

This class implements Shrunk security measures and its corresponding
verification system.

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_pending_role_requests
documentation:
Get all pending role requests for a role

```
param role

The internal name for the role

returns

A list of pending role requests, where each request is formatted as follows:
```

```json
{
    "role": "string",
    "entity": "string",
    "title": "string",
    "employee_types": ["string", "string", ...],
    "comment": "string",
    "time_requested": DateTime,
}
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_pending_role_requests_count
documentation:
Get the count of all pending role requests for a role

```
param role

Role requested

returns

The count of pending role requests
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_pending_role_request_for_entity
documentation:
Get a single pending role requests for a role and entity

```
param role

Role requested

param entity

The NetID of the user requesting the role

returns

A pending role request in the form
```

```json
{
    "role": "string",
    "entity": "string",
    "comment": "string",
    "time_requested": DateTime,
}
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.create_role_request
documentation:
Request a role for an entity

```
param role

Role to request

param entity

The NetID of the user requesting the role

The comment the user provided with their request
```

documentation:
Delete a role request and remember who did it. Delete the request from the database.

```
param role

Role to request

param entity

The NetID of the user requesting the role

param comment

The comment the admin should provide with their decision
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_role_request_text
documentation:
Get the text-related attributes needed for messages, emails, and forms.

```
param role

The internal name for the role
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.send_role_request_mail
documentation:
Send an email. If the type is 'confirmation', an email is sent to the user confirming that a role request has been sent to be manually processed. If the type is 'notification', an email is sent to the OSS team notifying them that a role request has been made. If the type is 'approval', an email is sent to the user confirming that their role request is approved along with a comment. If the type is 'denial', an email is sent to the user confirming that their role request is denied along with a comment.

```
param mail

The mail object

param type

The type of email to send

param role

The role requested

param entity

The NetID of the user requesting the role

param comment

Comment from the admin (only required for 'approval' and 'denial' types)
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_emails_enabled
documentation: Get the value of the emails_enabled attribute. This is to adjust the modal message for the user after they submit a role request.

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.get_slack_integration_enabled
documentation: Get the value of the slack_integration_enabled attribute. This is to adjust the modal message for the user after they submit a role request.

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.generate_role_request_component
documentation:
Generate the role request component needed for shrunk to send a formatted, interactive Slack message.

Args:
\:param role: The role name being requested
\:param entity: NetID of the requesting user

Returns:
\:returns: A dictionary containing the blocks needed for the Slack message and a plaintext message. The `blocks` field of the message should be initialized to the `blocks` attribute and the `text` field respectively.

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.send_role_request_notification_slack
documentation:
Send a message to the OSS team's Slack channel notifying them that a role request has been made. This should also update the time_requested field of the role request to the timestamp of the slack message.

```
param role

The role name being requested

param entity

NetID of the requesting user
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.role_requests.RoleRequestClient.delete_role_request_notification_slack
documentation:
Delete the role request notification message from the OSS team's Slack channel. This occurs either if the role request was handled within shrunk or handled within Slack.

```
param role

The role name being requested

param entity

NetID of the requesting user

param approved

Whether the role request was approved or denied
```
This class implements the Shrunk role request system

methods.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.get_long_url
documentation:
Given a short URL, returns the long URL.

Performs a case-insensitive search for the corresponding long URL.

```
param short_url

A shortened URL

returns

The long URL, or None if the short URL does not exist.
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.visit
documentation:
Visits the given URL and logs visit information.

On visiting a URL, this is guaranteed to perform at least the following
side effects if the short URL is valid:

> - Increment the hit counter
> - Log the visitor

If the URL is invalid, no side effects will occur.

```
param short_url

The short URL visited

param tracking_id

The contents of the visitor's tracking cookie, if any

param source_ip

The client's IP address

param user_agent

The client's user agent

param referer

The client's referer
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.get_visitor_id
documentation:
Gets a unique, opaque identifier for an IP address.

```
param ipaddr

a string containing an IPv4 address.

returns

A hexadecimal string which uniquely identifies the given IP address.
```

methods.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient._generate_unique_key
documentation: Generates a unique key.

methods.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient._base_encode
documentation:
Encodes an integer into our arbitrary link alphabet.

Given an integer, convert it to base-36. Letters are case-insensitive;
this function uses uppercase arbitrarily.

```
param integer

An integer.

returns

A string composed of characters from .
```

class_variables.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.ALPHABET
documentation: The alphabet used for encoding short urls.

class_variables.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.URL_MIN
documentation:
The shortest allowable URL.

This is the value of '1000' in the URL base encoding. Guarantees that all
URLs are at least four characters long.

class_variables.name: shrunk.client.links.LinksClient.URL_MAX
documentation:
The longest allowable URL.

This is the value of 'zzzzzzzz' in the URL base encoding. Guarantees that
all URLs do not exceed eight characters.

A class for database interactions. This class defines core
database-manipulation methods. Other methods are defined in the
mixins classes from which this class inherits.

modules.name: shrunk.api.user
documentation: Implement API endpoints under ``/api/user``

functions.name: shrunk.api.user.get_all_users
documentation:
POST /api/v1/user

Args:
    netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
    client (ShrunkClient): the client object

Obtains all users in the system.

Request format:
.. code-block:: json

{
    "operations": [
        {
            "type": "str",
            "field": "str",
            "specification": "str",
            "filterString": "str",
        },
        ...
    ]
}

Response format:

.. code-block:: json

    { "users": [{
            "netid": str,
            "organizations": [str, ...],
            "roles": [str, ...],
            "linksCreated": int,
        }, ...]
    }

functions.name: shrunk.api.user.get_user_system_options
documentation:
GET /api/v1/user/options

Args:
    netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
    client (ShrunkClient): the client object

Obtains options related to the shrunk user system.

Response format:

functions.name: shrunk._init_shrunk_client
documentation:
Connect to the database specified in self.config.
self.logger must be initialized before this function is called.

classes.name: shrunk.ObjectIdConverter
documentation:
URL converter for BSON object IDs, which we commonly use
as canonical IDs for objects.

classes.name: shrunk.Base32Converter
documentation:
URL converter to handle base32-encoded strings. This is useful
since Apache apparently has problems with urlencoded-slashes.

modules.name: shrunk.api.position
documentation: Implement API endpoints under ``/api/position``

functions.name: shrunk.api.position.get_position_info
documentation:
`GET /api/position/<b32:entity>`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  entity (str): the netid of the user to get position info for

Get the position info for a user. Response format:

```json
{
    "uid": List[str],
    "rutgersEduStaffDepartment": List[str],
    "title": List[str],
    "employeeType": List[str],
}
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.promote
documentation:
`PATCH /api/v1/security/promote`

Promotes a pending link to an actual link, creating a link document in
the link collection.

```
param link_id

id of link to promote
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.reject
documentation:
`PATCH /api/v1/security/patch`

Rejects a pending link, denying link creation in link collection forever.

```
param link_id

id of link to reject
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.security_test
documentation:
``GET /api/v1/security/security_test/<b32:long_url>``

This endpoint is meant for testing purposes only; it should only be called in the unit tests.
The purpose of this endpoint is to modularize testing of the security measures. In the case
that the security measures do not work, this test will be the first to clearly show that.

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.get_pending_links
documentation:
``GET /api/v1/security/pending_links``

Retrieves a list of pending links

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.get_pending_link_count
documentation:
``GET /api/v1/security/pending_links/count``

Retrieves the length of the list of pending links

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.get_link_status
documentation:
`GET /api/v1/security/status/<ObjectId:link_id>`

Gets the status of a pending link by id.
\:param link_id:

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.toggle_security
documentation:
``PATCH /api/v1/security/toggle``

Toggles whether or not security measures are on

functions.name: shrunk.api.security.get_security_status
documentation:
``GET /api/v1/security/get_status``

Checks the status of security measures

modules.name: shrunk.api.search
documentation: Implements endpoints under ``/api/search``

functions.name: shrunk.api.search.post_search_urls
documentation:
`POST /api/search`

Execute a search query. Request format:

```json
{
  "query?": "string",
  "set": {
    "set": "'user' | 'shared' | 'all' | 'org'",
    "org?": "string"
  },
  "show_expired_links": "boolean",
  "show_deleted_links": "boolean",
  "sort": {
    "key": "'created_time' | 'title' | 'visits' | 'relevance'",
    "order": "'ascending' | 'descending'"
  },
  "pagination?": {
    "skip": "number",
    "limit": "number"
  },
  "begin_time?": "date-time",
  "end_time?": "date-time"
}
```

Response format:

```json
{
   "count": "number",
   "results": [ {
     "id": "string",
     "title": "string",
     "long_url": "string",
     "created_time": "date-time",
     "expiration_time": "date-time | null",
     "visits": "number",
     "unique_visits": "number",
     "owner": "string",
     "aliases": [ { "alias": "string", "deleted": "boolean" } ],
     "is_expired": "boolean",
     "deletion_info?": {
       "deleted_by": "string",
       "deleted_time": "date-time"
     }
   } ]
}
```

```
param netid



param client



param req

```

```

```
param netid



param client

```

modules.name: shrunk.api.role_request
documentation: Implement API endpoints under ``/api/role_request``

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.get_role_requests_enabled
documentation:
`GET /api/role_request/role_requests_enabled`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object

Get the status of the role_requests_enabled flag. Response format:

```json
{ "role_requests_enabled": bool }
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.get_pending_role_requests
documentation:
`GET /api/role_request/<role>`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  role (str): the role to get pending requests for

Obtains all pending role requests for a role. Response format:

```json
{ "requests": [{
       "role": str,
       "entity": str,
       "comment": str,
       "time_requested": DateTime,
     }, ...]
}
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.get_pending_role_requests_count
documentation:
`GET /api/role_request/<role>/count`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  role (str): the role to get pending requests count for

Obtains the number of pending role requests for a given role. Response format:

```json
{ "count": int }
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.get_pending_role_request_for_entity
documentation:
`GET /api/role_request/<role>/<b32:entity>`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  entity (str): the entity to get the pending role request for
  role (str): the role to get the pending request for

Obtains a single pending role request for a role and entity. Response format:

```json
{ "role": str,
   "entity": str,
   "comment": str,
   "time_requested": DateTime,
 }
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.make_role_request
documentation:
`POST /api/role_request`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object

Make a role request as the entity requesting the role.

The request should include a JSON body with the following format:

```json
{
    "role": str,
    "comment": str
}
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.approve_role_request
documentation:
`POST /api/role_request/approve`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  entity (str): the entity to approve the role to
  role (str): the role to approve

Approve a role request by granting the role, then deleting it.

The request should include a JSON body with the following format:

```json
{
    "role": str,
    "entity": str,
    "comment": str
}
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.deny_role_request
documentation:
`DELETE /api/role_request/deny`

Args:

: netid (str): the netid of the user logged in
  client (ShrunkClient): the client object
  entity (str): the entity to approve the role to
  role (str): the role to approve

Deny a role request by deleting it without granting.

The request should include a JSON body with the following format:

```json
{
    "role": str,
    "entity": str,
    "comment": str"
}
```

functions.name: shrunk.api.role_request.deny_role_request_slack

modules.name: shrunk.api.org
documentation: Implements API endpoints under ``/api/org``

functions.name: shrunk.api.org.get_orgs
documentation:
`POST /api/org/list`

Lists organizations. Request format:

```json
{ "which": "'user' | 'all'" }
```

where the `"which"` property specifies whether to return information about all organizations
or only organizations of which the requesting user is a member. Only administrators may use the `"all"`
option. Response format:

```json
{ "orgs": [ {
    "id": "string",
    "name": "string",
    "is_member": "boolean",
    "is_admin": "boolean",
    "timeCreated": "date-time",
    "members": [
      { "netid": "string", "timeCreated": "date-time", "is_admin": "boolean" }
    ]
  } ]
}
```

Where the top-level `"is_member"` and `"is_admin"` properties specify respectively whether the requesting
user is a member and/or an administrator of the organization.

```
param netid



param client



param req

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.org.post_org
documentation:
`POST /api/org`

Create a new organization. The requesting user is automatically an administrator of the
newly-created organization. Returns the ID of the created organization. Request format:

```json
{ "name": "string" }
```

Response format:

```json
{ "id": "string" }
```

```
param netid



param client



param req

modules.name: shrunk.api.linkhub

functions.name: shrunk.api.linkhub.get_linkhub_by_alias
documentation: Gives the end-user less information on what the document contains.

functions.name: shrunk.util.string.get_domain
documentation:
Takes in a url and gets the top level domain,
e.g. `https://lmao-d.f.foo.rutgers.edu/lmao.php?thing=true` becomes `rutgers.edu`

```
param long_url

The URL from which to extract the domain
```

functions.name: shrunk.util.string.validate_url
documentation:
stolen from python-validators

This validator is based on the wonderful [URL validator of dperini].

Examples:

```
>>> url('http://foobar.dk')
True

>>> url('ftp://foobar.dk')
True

>>> url('http://10.0.0.1')
True

>>> url('http://foobar.d')
False
```

:::{versionadded} 0.2
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.2
Added support for various exotic URLs and fixed various false
positives.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.3
Added `public` parameter.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.11.0
Made the regular expression this function uses case insensitive.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.11.3
Added support for URLs containing localhost
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.0.0
Removed unused `public` parameter.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.3.0
Return `False` instead of raising an exception.
:::

```
param value

URL address string to validate

returns

Whether or not the URL was valid
```

[url validator of dperini]: https://gist.github.com/dperini/729294

functions.name: shrunk.util.verification.verify_signature
documentation:
Verify that the payload was sent from GitHub by validating SHA256.

Raise and return 403 if not authorized.

Args:
    payload_body: original request body to verify (request.body())
    secret_token: GitHub app webhook token (WEBHOOK_SECRET)
    signature_header: header received from GitHub (x-hub-signature-256)

functions.name: shrunk.util.ldap.is_valid_netid
documentation: Return True if the netid is valid, false otherwise.

functions.name: shrunk.util.ldap.query_position_info
documentation:
Query LDAP for position info of a given netid. Return a dictionary of attributes.

Args:
    netid (str): The netid of the user to query position info for.

Returns:
    Dict[str, List[str]]: A dictionary containing the position information of the user.
        The keys of the dictionary represent the attribute names, and the values are lists
        of attribute values.

functions.name: shrunk.util.decorators.require_api_key
documentation:
Decorator to require an API key in the headers of the request.

Args:

Returns:
    Any: the decorated function

modules.name: shrunk.sso
documentation: Shibboleth authentication.

functions.name: shrunk.sso.login
documentation:
Get the user's attributes from shibboleth, decide whether the user
should be allowed to access Shrunk, and possibly grant roles. The
roles system must be initialized before this function is called.

modules.name: shrunk.local-test-config
documentation:
shrunk - Rutgers University URL Shortener

Configuration options for shrunk. This is an example file; modify this and save
it as config.py.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.DB_HOST
documentation: The host machine for the database.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.DB_PORT
documentation: The database's port on the host machine.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.GEOLITE_PATH
documentation: The path to the geolite geoip database.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.SECRET_KEY
documentation: A secret key for Flask.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.SHRUNK_URL
documentation: The public URL for shrunk.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.LINKSERVER_URL
documentation: The public URL for the link server.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.DUAL_SERVER
documentation:
Determines whether or not the web application server doubles as the link
server. The default setting is False, which assumes that a separate server
handles the links.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.USER_WHITELIST
documentation:
These users are always permitted to login (i.e. they can't be blacklisted)
and are automatically admins.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.SSO_ATTRIBUTE_MAP
documentation: Map SSO attributes to session keys

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.SSO_LOGIN_URL
documentation: URL for local shibboleth login

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.LOG_FORMAT
documentation: The format for the logger.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.LOG_FILENAME
documentation: The name of the log file.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.MAX_DISPLAY_LINKS
documentation: The maximum number of links to display per page.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.MAX_VISITS_FOR_CSV
documentation:
The maximum number of visits to allow when generating a CSV file from search
results. If a CSV would have more than this number of visits, an error is returned.
This restriction does not apply to CSV files generated from a single short URL.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.LDAP_VALIDATE_NETIDS
documentation: Whether to query the LDAP server to validate netids.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.RESERVED_WORDS
documentation:
Words that cannot be used as shortened URLs. Names used as routes are automatically
disallowed, and do not need to be added to this list.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.SECURITY_MEASURES_ON
documentation:
Whether the security measures are turned on or off.
You would this to be set to True when performing unit tests
or you'll fail the security tests. However, in prod it can be whatever.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.GOOGLE_SAFE_BROWSING_API
documentation: A secret key for Google Cloud Web Risk API

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.TRACKING_PIXEL_UI_ENABLED
documentation: Temporary flag to enable or disable the tracking pixel creation feature on user's dashboard.

variables.name: shrunk.local-test-config.LINKHUB_INTEGRATION_ENABLED
documentation: Configuration value to enable or disable dynamically generated web pages that contains URLs.

modules.name: shrunk.api.link
documentation: Implements API endpoints under ``/api/link``

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.create_link
documentation:
`POST /api/link`

Create a new link. Returns id of created link or errors. Request format:

```json
{ "title": "string", "long_url": "string",
  "expiration_time": "2020-11-11T11:11:11Z",
  "editors": ["<ACL_ENTRY>"], "viewers": ["<ACL_ENTRY>"]}
```

an ACL entry looks like. for orgs the id must be a valid bson ObjectId

```json
{"_id": "netid|org_id", "type": "org|user"}
```

Success response format:

```json
{ "id": "string" }
```

Error response format:

```json
{ "errors": ["long_url"] }
```

```
param netid



param client



param req

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.validate_long_url
documentation:
`GET /api/validate_long_url/<b32:long_url>`

Validate a long URL. This endpoint is used for form validation in the frontend. Response format:

```json
{ "valid": "boolean", "reason?": "string" }
```

```
param netid



param client



param long_url

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>`

Get information about a link. Basically just returns the Mongo document.

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_by_title
documentation:
`GET /api/link/search_by_title/<title>`

Finds information of a single link by exact title. This simple method was made for
security unit tests. This method is NOT mean to be a comprehensive endpoint
called upon in production.

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.modify_link
documentation:
`PATCH /api/link/<link_id>`

Modify an existing link. Returns 204 on success or 403 on error. Request format:

```json
{ "title?": "string", "long_url?": "string", "expiration_time?": "string | null" }
```

Properties present in the request will be set. Properties missing from the request will not
be modified. If `"expiration_time"` is present and set to `null`, the effect is to remove
the link's expiration time.

```
param netid



param client



param req



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.modify_acl
documentation:
`PATCH /api/link/<link_id>/acl`

Modify an existing link's acl. Returns 204 on success or 403 on error.
Request format:

```json
{ "action": "add|remove", "entry": "<ACL_ENTRY>",
  "acl": "editors|viewers" }
```

an ACL entry looks like. for orgs the id must be a valid bson ObjectId

```json
{ "_id": "netid|org_id", "type": "org|netid" }
```

```
param netid



param client



param req



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_visits
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>/visits`

Get anonymized visit data associated with a link. Response format:

```json
{ "visits": [ {
    "link_id": "string",
    "alias": "string",
    "visitor_id": "string",
    "user_agent": "string",
    "referer": "string",
    "state_code": "string",
    "country_code": "string",
    "time": "date-time"
} ] }
```

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_overall_stats
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>/stats`

Get overall stats associated with a link. Response format:

```json
{ "total_visits": "number", "unique_visits": "number" }
```

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_visit_stats
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>/stats/visits`

Get daily visits information associated with a link. Response format:

```json
{ "visits": [ {
    "_id": { "year": "number", "month": "number", "day": "number" },
    "all_visits": "number",
    "first_time_visits": "number"
} ] }
```

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_geoip_stats
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>/stats/geoip`

Get GeoIP stats associated with a link. Response format:

```json
{
  "us": [ { "code": "string", "value": "number" } ],
  "world": [ { "code": "string", "value": "number" } ]
}
```

where the value of `"code"` is an ISO country or subdivison code and the value of `"value"` is the
number of visits in that geographic region.

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.get_link_browser_stats
documentation:
`GET /api/link/<link_id>/stats/browser`

Get stats about browsers and referers of visitors. Response format:

```json
{
  "browsers": [ { "name": "string", "y": "number" } ],
  "platforms": [ { "name": "string", "y": "number" } ],
  "referers": [ { "name": "string", "y": "number" } ]
}
```

```
param netid



param client



param link_id

```

functions.name: shrunk.api.link.create_alias
documentation:
`POST /api/link/<link_id>/alias`

Create a new alias for a link. Returns the created alias or an error. Request format:

```json
{ "alias?": "string", "description?": "string", "extension?" : "string" }
```

If the `"alias"` field is omitted, the server will generate a random alias. If the `"description"` field is
omitted, it will default to the empty string. Success response format:

```json
{ "alias": "string" }
```

Error response format:

```json
{ "errors": ["alias"] }
```

```
param netid



param client



param req



param link_id

```
